📊 FinCalc Lab
Calculators Guides About

Financial answers in seconds

71 free calculators for loans, savings, retirement, taxes and debt — each with a plain-English explanation of the math behind the result. No sign-up. Nothing tracked. Everything runs in your browser.

71 calculators 31 in-depth guides 100% free & private

All calculators

No calculators match — try another word.

Savings & Investing

📈 Compound Interest Calculator Project how savings grow with monthly deposits and compounding. 🎯 Savings Goal Calculator Find the exact monthly saving needed to hit a target amount. 🌴 Retirement Savings Calculator Project your nest egg and the income it could support. 📊 Investment Return (CAGR) Calculator Turn a total gain into an honest annualized return. 💹 ROI Calculator Total and annualized return on any investment or project. ⏳ Rule of 72 Calculator Estimate doubling time from an interest or growth rate. 🕐 Present Value Calculator Discount a future sum back to today's dollars. 🪙 Dividend Income Calculator Annual dividend income, yield and reinvested growth. 📐 Simple Interest Calculator Interest on principal only, no compounding. 🏦 CD Calculator Maturity value and interest earned on a CD. 🔁 APY Calculator Turn a nominal rate + compounding into true APY. 📉 Real Rate of Return Calculator What your return is really worth after inflation. 💰 Millionaire Calculator Years to reach $1,000,000 (or any target). 🔮 Future Value Calculator Grow a lump sum plus deposits to a future value. 🪤 Investment Fee Impact Calculator How much a fund's annual fee costs you over time. ☕ Latte Factor Calculator What a small recurring spend could grow to if invested.

Retirement

🏦 401(k) Calculator Project 401(k) growth including the employer match. 💰 Roth IRA Calculator Project tax-free Roth IRA growth from regular contributions. 🏖️ Retirement Withdrawal Calculator How long a nest egg lasts at a given withdrawal. 💵 Annuity Payout Calculator Monthly income a lump sum can pay over a period. 🔥 FIRE Number Calculator Your target nest egg for financial independence. 🏄 Coast FIRE Calculator The amount that grows to retirement with no new savings.

Loans & Debt

💳 Loan Payment Calculator Monthly payment, total interest and cost of any amortized loan. 🏠 Mortgage Calculator Full monthly housing cost: loan, property tax and insurance. 🚀 Loan Payoff Calculator See how extra monthly payments shorten a loan and cut interest. 🧊 Debt Avalanche vs Snowball Calculator Compare the two classic payoff strategies on your own debts. 🚗 Car Affordability Calculator Turn a monthly budget into a realistic car price. 💳 Credit Card Payoff Calculator How long to clear a card, and what interest costs you. ⚖️ Debt-to-Income (DTI) Calculator The ratio lenders use to approve mortgages and loans. 🔑 House Down Payment Calculator Down payment amount and monthly saving to reach it. 🔄 Mortgage Refinance Calculator Monthly savings and break-even point of refinancing. 🎓 Student Loan Calculator Monthly payment and total interest on student debt. 🗝️ Car Lease Calculator Estimate a monthly lease payment from the key terms. 🏡 Extra Mortgage Payment Calculator How extra payments cut mortgage interest and time. 🗓️ Biweekly Mortgage Calculator How biweekly payments shorten your mortgage. 📅 Amortization Schedule Calculator Yearly principal vs interest breakdown of any loan. 🧮 Debt Consolidation Calculator See if one consolidated loan beats your current debts. 🏦 Home Equity Calculator Your home equity and borrowable amount at a target LTV. 🏡 Home Affordability Calculator Max home price from your income using the 28/36 rule. 📍 Mortgage Points Calculator Whether paying points to lower your rate pays off. ⚖️ Loan Comparison Calculator Put two loan offers side by side to see the real cost.

Income & Budgeting

💰 Salary to Hourly Calculator Convert annual salary to hourly, weekly and monthly pay. 🧾 Take-Home Pay Estimator Estimate net monthly pay from gross salary and tax rates. 💸 50/30/20 Budget Calculator Split your take-home pay into needs, wants and savings. 🛟 Emergency Fund Calculator Size your emergency fund and see how long it takes to build. 🎈 Inflation Calculator See what today's money will be worth in the future. 📋 Net Worth Calculator Add up what you own, subtract what you owe. 📆 Hourly to Salary Calculator Convert an hourly wage into weekly, monthly and yearly pay. 🏡 Rent Affordability Calculator A sensible rent range based on your income. ⏰ Overtime Pay Calculator Total weekly pay including overtime at any multiplier. 💵 Paycheck Calculator Gross and net pay per paycheck by pay frequency. 📈 Pay Raise Calculator New pay after a raise, and if it beats inflation. 💹 Savings Rate Calculator Your savings rate and what it means for freedom. 📈 Rent Increase Calculator New rent after an increase, and if it's fair. 📉 Salary Inflation Calculator Whether your salary has kept pace with inflation.

Taxes & Shopping

🧾 Sales Tax Calculator Add sales tax to a price, or back it out of a total. 🏷️ Discount Calculator Final price after a percentage off, and what you save. 🍽️ Tip Calculator Work out the tip and split a bill between people. 🧾 VAT Calculator Add VAT to a net price or extract it from a gross price.

Business

⚖️ Break-Even Point Calculator How many sales cover your costs — the first business question. 📉 Profit Margin Calculator Margin, markup and profit from cost and price. 🧑‍💻 Freelance Hourly Rate Calculator Turn a target income into the hourly rate you must charge. 🏷️ Markup Calculator Set a selling price from cost and desired markup. 🎯 LTV : CAC Ratio Calculator Lifetime value vs acquisition cost — is growth healthy? 🚀 Startup Runway Calculator Months of cash left from burn rate and balance. ⏳ Payback Period Calculator Time for an investment to recoup its cost. 📐 EBITDA Calculator EBITDA and margin from your income statement. 🏘️ Rental Property ROI Calculator Cap rate, cash flow and cash-on-cash return.

Everyday Math

🔢 Percentage Calculator Three everyday percentage questions in one tool. ⛽ Fuel Cost Calculator Cost of a trip, and yearly fuel spend. 🛒 Unit Price Calculator Find the real cost per unit and the better deal.

Guides & explainers

"What Is a 401(k) Match, and Why It's the Best Deal in Finance" How employer 401(k) matching works, why skipping it means leaving free money on the table, and how vesting and contribution limits affect you. "The 50/30/20 Rule in Real Life: Where It Works, Where It Breaks, How to Fix It" An honest field guide to the most famous budgeting rule — including what to do when rent alone eats 45% of your income. "APR vs APY: The Difference That Costs (or Earns) You Money" APR and APY look alike but mean different things. Understand compounding, why lenders quote APR and banks quote APY, and how to compare fairly. How Compound Interest Builds Wealth (and Why Starting Late Costs So Much) A plain-English walkthrough of compound growth, with real numbers showing why time in the market beats timing — and what starting five years late actually costs. "What Actually Affects Your Credit Score" The five factors behind your credit score, which ones matter most, and the practical habits that raise it — without the myths. "Dollar-Cost Averaging: Why Boring, Automatic Investing Wins" How dollar-cost averaging works, what the research actually says versus lump-sum investing, and why the biggest benefit is behavioral, not mathematical. "Emergency Fund vs Investing: Which Dollar Goes Where First?" A decision framework for the classic dilemma — cash safety versus market growth — including the order of operations most planners actually recommend. "The FIRE Movement Explained: Financial Independence by the Numbers" What FIRE really means, the 25x rule and 4% withdrawal, the variants (Lean, Fat, Coast, Barista FIRE), and the one number that decides your timeline. "Good Debt vs Bad Debt: A Simple Framework for Borrowing Decisions" Not all debt is equal. A practical framework for telling wealth-building debt from wealth-destroying debt, using interest rate, asset value and cash flow. "How Much House Can You Afford? The 28/36 Rule and Its Honest Limits" A practical framework for setting your housing budget — the 28/36 rule, the costs first-time buyers forget, and when the guidelines mislead. "How Much Should You Save for Retirement?" Rules of thumb, the real math behind retirement targets, and how to turn a scary abstract goal into a monthly number you can actually act on. "How Much Should You Spend on a Car? Rules of Thumb That Actually Work" Practical guidelines for a sensible car budget — the 20/4/10 rule, why total cost dwarfs the sticker price, and how a car quietly drains wealth. "How to Build Credit From Scratch (and Fix a Low Score)" A practical, step-by-step guide to building a credit history from zero and raising a low score — what actually moves the needle and what to ignore. "How to Make a Budget That Actually Sticks" A practical guide to budgeting that survives real life — the main methods compared, how to set one up in an afternoon, and why most budgets fail. "How to Negotiate a Raise (Without the Awkwardness)" A practical, evidence-based guide to asking for a raise — how to build your case, what number to name, the words to use, and why it compounds over a career. "Index Funds Explained: Why the Boring Choice Beats the Pros" What an index fund is, why it quietly outperforms most active managers after fees, and how to use one as the core of a simple, low-cost portfolio. "Lifestyle Creep: Why Earning More Doesn't Make You Richer" How lifestyle inflation quietly eats every raise, why high earners can still live paycheck to paycheck, and a simple rule to keep more of what you earn. "Marginal vs Effective Tax Rate: The Misunderstanding That Costs People Real Money" Why "being in the 24% bracket" doesn't mean losing 24% of your income, how progressive brackets actually work, and the raise myth debunked with numbers. "Why Net Worth Is the Only Number That Really Matters" Income tells you how fast you're rowing; net worth tells you where the boat actually is. How to calculate it, track it, and use it to build wealth. "How to Pay Off Debt Faster: A Strategy Guide That Respects Both Math and Psychology" Avalanche, snowball, consolidation and balance transfers compared honestly — which debt payoff strategy fits your numbers and your temperament. "How to Price Freelance Work Without Underselling Yourself" Why new freelancers charge too little, how to calculate a rate that actually sustains you, and when to move from hourly to value-based pricing. "Renting vs Buying a Home: The Honest Math" Beyond 'rent is throwing money away' — the real costs on both sides, the break-even horizon, and how to decide based on your actual situation. "Roth vs Traditional: Which Retirement Account Wins?" A clear framework for choosing between Roth and Traditional retirement accounts — the tax logic, the break-even rule, and why many people use both. "Sequence of Returns Risk: Why When You Retire Matters as Much as How Much" The retirement danger hiding inside average returns — why a crash early in retirement is far worse than the same crash later, and how to defend against it. "Sinking Funds: The Simple Trick for Big Expenses" How sinking funds turn irregular, budget-wrecking expenses into small predictable monthly savings — and why they beat relying on your emergency fund. "How to Start Investing With Little Money: A Beginner's Roadmap" You don't need thousands to start investing. A practical, jargon-free roadmap for beginners — what to do first, what to buy, and the mistakes to skip. "Stocks vs Bonds: How to Think About Asset Allocation" What stocks and bonds actually are, how they behave differently, and how to choose a stock/bond mix that matches your timeline and stomach for risk. "The Time Value of Money: Why a Dollar Today Beats a Dollar Tomorrow" The single most important idea in finance, explained plainly — present value, future value, discounting, and why it underpins every investment and loan decision. "The True Cost of Owning a Car (It's Not the Payment)" Depreciation, insurance, fuel, maintenance and financing — the full cost of car ownership, and how to spend far less on getting around. "Understanding Inflation: How It Quietly Shrinks Your Money" What inflation is, why central banks target it, how it erodes cash and helps borrowers, and how to protect your money from losing value over time. "When Does Refinancing Your Mortgage Make Sense?" How to tell whether refinancing will save you money — the break-even rule, the costs people forget, and the traps that make a lower rate a worse deal.

Why FinCalc Lab?

Private by design

Every calculation happens in your browser. Your salary, debts and savings never leave your device.

The math, explained

Each tool shows the formula it uses and walks through what the result actually means for you.

No paywalls, ever

Free to use, no accounts, no "premium" upsell. Supported by unobtrusive advertising.

FinCalc Lab

Free, fast and honest financial calculators. All tools run entirely in your browser — we never see or store your numbers.

About Contact Privacy Policy Terms of Use

© 2026 FinCalc Lab. All calculators and articles are for educational purposes only and do not constitute financial advice. Consult a qualified professional before making financial decisions.
